title: Golden Brown Saudi Arabian Lamb Flatbread
description: A traditional Saudi Arabian dish featuring lamb, dried cherries, and a mirepoix base, perfect for a midnight craving. This recipe is anti-inflammatory friendly and makes a delicious snack.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the yeast and 1 cup of warm water, letting it sit for 5 minutes until the yeast is activated.
Add the flour, salt, and black pepper to the bowl, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, covering it with a damp cloth, and letting it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat, adding the chopped onion, carrot, and celery, and cooking until they are softened.
Add the lamb pieces to the pan, cooking until they are browned on all sides.
Stir in the dried cherries and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, placing a quarter of the lamb mixture in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the flatbread.
Brush the tops of the flatbreads with olive oil and bake in a preheated oven at 400 degrees for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
